---
title: "Research"
---

::: {.page-lead}
How circuits and internal states shape what an animal does, and the light-driven tools I build
to switch those circuits on and off in the fly brain.
:::

[Selected research]{.eyebrow}

::::: {.card-grid}

:::: {.pcard .is-soon}
<div class="pcard-ph">Coming soon</div>

::: {.pcard-body}
### Neural circuits of behavior in *Drosophila*
[PhD thesis · first-author · unpublished data]{.paper-meta}

A large-scale optogenetic and behavioral ethomics study of how brain circuits shape movement
in *Drosophila*, forming the core of my PhD thesis.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" src="papers/opn3/graphical-abstract.png" alt="Graphical abstract of the OPN3 silencing paper">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [The silence after the light](papers/opn3/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[co-first author · preprint · bioRxiv, 2026]{.paper-meta}

A bistable opto-GPCR that lets us silence *Drosophila* neurons for minutes with a single brief
flash of green light, providing a low-toxicity optogenetic tool for studying neural circuits and behavior.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard .is-soon}
<div class="pcard-ph">Coming soon</div>

::: {.pcard-body}
### DABEST 2.0: estimation statistics
[co-author · in revision, Nature Methods, 2026]{.paper-meta}

Estimation graphics for effect sizes instead of p-values. I helped develop DABEST's proportion
plot for binary data and carried out the literature survey that motivated it.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" src="papers/kcr/kcr-06.png" alt="Figure 5 C. elegans results from the KCR paper">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [Kalium channelrhodopsins](papers/kcr/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[co-author · Nature Communications, 2024]{.paper-meta}

As part of a cross-species evaluation of kalium channelrhodopsins (KCRs) as potassium-selective
optogenetic silencers, I led the *C. elegans* behavioral studies, building the assay, tracking
system, and testing pipeline.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" src="papers/chromosomes/figure1.jpg" alt="Figure 1 from the chromosomes-in-fused-cells paper">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [Separating chromosomes in fused cells](papers/chromosomes/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[co-author · Communications Biology, 2022]{.paper-meta}

How fused *Drosophila* cells keep two genomes separate through asymmetric chromatin retention
and nuclear envelope boundaries.
:::

::::

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" src="papers/sibling/graphical-abstract.jpg" alt="Graphical abstract of the sibling cell size asymmetry paper">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [Sibling cell size asymmetry](papers/sibling/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[co-author · iScience, 2019]{.paper-meta}

Investigating how hydrostatic pressure and cortical actomyosin work together to produce two
unequal-sized daughter cells during neural stem cell division.
:::

::::

:::::
